title: Sales Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the actual distribution or movement of a product or service to the customer. Coordinate sales distribution by establishing sales territories, quotas, and goals and establish training programs for sales representatives. Analyze sales statistics gathered by staff to determine sales potential and inventory requirements and monitor the preferences of customers.

profiles.description: Oversee regional and local sales managers and their staffs.
profiles.rationale: This requires human leadership judgment to “oversee … sales managers and their staffs,” and Gartner/SiriusDecisions studies show front‑line sales managers spend several hours weekly on coaching/supervision (~4 hours).

profiles.description: Resolve customer complaints regarding sales and service.
profiles.rationale: Escalations to “resolve customer complaints regarding sales and service” involve sensitive, liability‑bearing conversations, and ICMI contact center benchmarks place escalated case handling around 20–30 minutes per case.

profiles.description: Monitor customer preferences to determine focus of sales efforts.
profiles.rationale: An AI agent can automatically “monitor customer preferences” and surface insights, and Gartner marketing analytics benchmarks indicate managers typically spend ~1 hour weekly on such reviews without automation.

profiles.description: Confer with potential customers regarding equipment needs, and advise customers on types of equipment to purchase.
profiles.rationale: Because the manager must “confer with potential customers … and advise,” AI can prep discovery questions and proposals but a human leads the consult, and Gartner sales-cycle data suggest ~60–90 minutes including prep and follow‑up per client consult.

profiles.description: Review operational records and reports to project sales and determine profitability.
profiles.rationale: Standardized analytics can auto “review operational records and reports to project sales and determine profitability,” and Gartner/SiriusDecisions indicate managers otherwise spend ~2 hours weekly on reporting/forecast reviews.

profiles.description: Plan and direct staffing, training, and performance evaluations to develop and control sales and service programs.
profiles.rationale: This includes “staffing … and performance evaluations,” which require managerial authority and HR judgment, and SHRM benchmarks show line managers typically invest 2–4 hours per hire for staffing decisions/training plans.

profiles.description: Direct and coordinate activities involving sales of manufactured products, services, commodities, real estate, or other subjects of sale.
profiles.rationale: AI can coordinate schedules, trackers, and status for “direct and coordinate activities involving sales,” but humans still decide tradeoffs, and Gartner research shows ~1–2 hours of coordination per active territory/program.

profiles.description: Determine price schedules and discount rates.
profiles.rationale: Setting “price schedules and discount rates” is judgmental, so AI can model scenarios while a human approves, and Gartner B2B pricing studies indicate a few hours of work per pricing update.

profiles.description: Prepare budgets and approve budget expenditures.
profiles.rationale: The task includes “approve budget expenditures,” which is a fiduciary decision requiring human sign‑off, and Gartner budgeting benchmarks show department managers spend ~3–6 hours per budget cycle per cost center.

profiles.description: Confer or consult with department heads to plan advertising services and to secure information on equipment and customer specifications.
profiles.rationale: This is a direct meeting to “confer or consult with department heads,” where AI can prepare agendas and notes but a human conducts the consult, and Gartner cross‑functional meeting norms are ~60 minutes.

profiles.description: Visit franchised dealers to stimulate interest in establishment or expansion of leasing programs.
profiles.rationale: To “visit franchised dealers” requires physical presence and persuasive interaction, and Gartner field‑sales studies show onsite calls typically take ~1.5–2 hours including logistics.

profiles.description: Represent company at trade association meetings to promote products.
profiles.rationale: “Represent company at trade association meetings” involves in‑person networking and reputational responsibility unsuitable for automation, and Gartner event-marketing norms put sessions/networking at ~1–2 hours.

profiles.description: Advise dealers and distributors on policies and operating procedures to ensure functional effectiveness of business.
profiles.rationale: While AI can draft policy summaries and talking points, a human must “advise dealers and distributors,” and Gartner channel‑partner management benchmarks show 30–60 minute advisory calls.

profiles.description: Assess marketing potential of new and existing store locations, considering statistics and expenditures.
profiles.rationale: AI can analyze data to “assess marketing potential … considering statistics and expenditures,” but a human validates site decisions, and Gartner retail/site‑selection workflows typically take a few hours per location.

profiles.description: Direct, coordinate, and review sales and service accounting and record-keeping, as well as receiving and shipping.
profiles.rationale: Workflow automations can execute and summarize “direct, coordinate, and review … accounting and record‑keeping … receiving and shipping,” and Gartner sales‑ops management indicates ~1–2 hours weekly is otherwise spent on these checks.

profiles.description: Direct clerical staff to keep records of export correspondence, bid requests, and credit collections, and to maintain current information on tariffs, licenses, and restrictions.
profiles.rationale: An AI ops agent can fully route and log “records of export correspondence, bid requests … tariffs, licenses, and restrictions,” and Forrester operations benchmarks show manual routing/entry typically takes ~5 minutes per item.

profiles.description: Direct foreign sales and service outlets of an organization.
profiles.rationale: For “direct[ing] foreign sales and service outlets,” AI can consolidate metrics and draft directives, while humans make strategic calls, and Gartner global sales management cadences often require 1–2 hours per outlet.
